game_name: Solar Jetman: Hunt for the Golden Warpship

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Low gravity environment for learning inertia-based control
Cable drag affects flight control; must manage weight vs maneuverability
Optional wormholes deeper in caverns require navigation skill
Reveals full cavern layout including items and wormholes
Each planet has unique gravitational pull; mastery requires relearning inertia
Vulnerable suit form requires quick return to mothership for new pod
Combat alongside flight and collection mechanics
Strategic spending on pod upgrades between missions
Each planet contains one piece; must return to mothership to progress
Must gather sufficient fuel from each planet to reach next destination

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to mothership with collected items
typical_tension_triggers: Gravity shift when entering new planet
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension based on resource management (fuel/items) combined with environmental gravity shifts; tension increases per planet as gravity becomes more extreme

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Wormholes deeper in caverns signal optional routes and hidden items; Super Mapping Device reveals all optional content
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Unused planet 'Dryn Kup Leez' accessible via Game Genie code; early Mapping Device access via password

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low to moderate; Planet 1 (Preludon) introduces core mechanics with minimal gravity challenge
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate to high; gravity variations increase significantly; enemy density in caverns escalates
difficulty_curve.late_game: High; extreme gravitational pulls make stable flight and precise maneuvering increasingly difficult; mastery of inertia physics required
notable_spikes: Mid-game planets with high gravity requiring relearned flight patterns
Late planets with extreme gravity creating inertia management challenges

notable_level_patterns.description: Each planet introduces unique gravitational pull requiring player adaptation. Constant pull changes every level, forcing re-mastery of inertia-based controls. No drag physics—pure momentum and gravity interaction.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Progression from Preludon (lower gravity tutorial) to high-gravity planets later in sequence

notable_level_patterns.description: Navigate enemy-infested caverns using tow cable to collect items, deposit in wormholes or return to mothership. Tow cable drag affects flight difficulty proportionally to collected weight.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All planets follow this core loop with gravity variations

notable_level_patterns.description: Must collect sufficient fuel from current planet AND Golden Warpship piece before advancing. Failure to find fuel creates soft reset via mothership return.
Pod destruction ejects Jetman into agile but vulnerable space suit form. Must navigate back to mothership in suit to retrieve new pod. Suit death = life loss.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Triggered by collision damage across all planets
